title: Aberdeen Group January 2001 Research Meeting
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
subject_domain: IT market research; research organization; analyst management; European expansion
abstract: Nine-slide presentation from Aberdeen Group's January 2001 Research Meeting documenting the research organization structure, Research Council and Product Council roles, VP Advanced R&D responsibilities, 2000 revenue performance and 2001 targets, and European operation status. Core Research hit $18.7M in 2000 with a $26.0M target for 2001. European Research generated over $0.5M in 2000 with $0.7M in operating losses; 2001 revenue target of $1.27M. Historical snapshot of Aberdeen at scale with many new analysts, just before the tech crash.
importance_rationale: Research organization snapshot at peak Aberdeen scale in January 2001; revenue data and org structure documented just before the dot-com crash; historically significant
relevance_rationale: Documents Aberdeen's actual financial targets, org hierarchy, and geographic expansion plans at a critical historical juncture
prescience_rationale: Emphasis on thought leadership and methodological consistency foreshadowed best practices in analyst firm management
